specification.topic-statement: The token count cache tracks per-component token costs for an agent's sliding context window.

scope.in-scope: Cache lifecycle (creation, cloning), per-component cost retrieval (system prompt, tool definitions, per-turn, total), known-cost recording, cache invalidation, structural operations (adding turns, pruning oldest turn, removing turns from a given index), budget configuration, and non-computing introspection.
scope.boundary: This topic submits a constructed request to the provider and receives either a token count integer or a failure indication. Requests are constructed from agent messages, model name, and system prompt text as part of this topic's computation; the counting itself is across this boundary.
This topic reads the agent's message list (role and content per message), current message count, model name, and effective system prompt. It does not modify the agent.

field.name: system prompt token cost
field.note: uncached sentinel when not yet known

field.name: tool definitions token cost
field.note: uncached sentinel when not yet known

field.name: per-turn token costs
field.note: each entry independently uncached or known; array grows dynamically

field.name: total token cost
field.note: uncached sentinel when not yet known

field.name: context start message index
field.note: index into agent message list of oldest active message

field.name: pruned turn count
field.note: cumulative count of turns removed from the front

field.name: type
field.note: text, tool call, tool result, thinking, or other

field.name: text content
field.note: present when type is text or thinking

field.name: tool call arguments
field.note: present when type is tool call

field.name: tool result content
field.note: present when type is tool result

behavior.creation: A new cache is created associated with a specific agent. All component costs — system prompt, tool definitions, and total — are initialized to uncached. The per-turn array is allocated with an initial capacity of 8 entries and a turn count of 0. The token budget defaults to 100,000. The context start message index is 0. The pruned turn count is 0.
behavior.cloning: A cache is cloned for a different agent (used when an agent is forked). The clone receives copies of all cached values from the source: system cost, tool cost, total cost, all per-turn costs, context start message index, pruned turn count, and token budget. If the source's turn array capacity is 0, the clone uses a capacity of 8; otherwise the source's capacity is used. The clone is associated with the new agent, not the source agent.
behavior.retrieving-system-tokens:
When the system token cost is requested:
If the system token cost is already known, it is returned immediately.
If uncached, the agent's effective system prompt text is retrieved. A request is constructed using the agent's model name and the system prompt. The request is submitted to the provider for token counting.
If the agent has no model name set, the string "default" is used.
If the provider returns a count, that count is stored as the system token cost, the total token cost is reset to uncached, and the count is returned.
If any step in provider computation fails — prompt retrieval fails, request construction fails, or the provider call fails — the system prompt's byte length is divided by 4 (integer division, truncating toward zero) and that value is returned.
The bytes-based estimate is not stored. The system token cost remains uncached, and the total token cost is not reset. On the next request for the system cost, the provider will be contacted again.
If the system prompt is null, the byte length is treated as 0 and 0 is returned.
behavior.retrieving-tool-tokens:
When the tool definitions token cost is requested:
If the tool token cost is already known, it is returned immediately.
If uncached, a request is constructed using only the agent's model name — no system prompt and no messages. The request is submitted to the provider for token counting.
If the agent has no model name set, the string "default" is used.
If the provider returns a count, that count is stored as the tool token cost, the total token cost is reset to uncached, and the count is returned.
If request construction or the provider call fails, 0 is returned.
The value 0 is not stored. The tool token cost remains uncached.
Unlike system and per-turn costs, the tool definitions fallback on failure is 0, not a bytes-based estimate.
behavior.retrieving-turn-tokens:
When the token cost of a specific turn is requested by its zero-based index:
If the requested index is greater than or equal to the active turn count, the process terminates abnormally.
If that turn's token cost is already known, it is returned immediately.
The boundaries of the turn within the agent's message list are found by iterating forward from the context start message index, counting user-role messages. Turn index N begins at the (N+1)th user-role message encountered and ends at the message immediately before the (N+2)th user-role message. The last active turn (with no subsequent user-role message) extends through the final message in the agent's list.
A request is constructed from the turn's messages using the agent's model name. The request is submitted to the provider for token counting.
If the provider returns a count, that count is stored as the turn's token cost, the total token cost is reset to uncached, and the count is returned.
If the provider call fails, the byte lengths of qualifying content across all messages in the turn are summed. The sum is divided by 4 (integer division, truncating toward zero) and returned. This value is not stored; the turn's cost remains uncached.
Qualifying content types and their contributing fields: - Text blocks: the text field, if not null - Tool call blocks: the arguments field, if not null - Tool result blocks: the content field, if not null - Thinking blocks: the text field, if not null
Content blocks of all other types contribute 0 bytes. Null fields contribute 0 bytes.
If no messages are found for the turn (turn bounds resolution yields nothing), 0 is returned.
behavior.retrieving-total-tokens:
When the total token cost is requested:
If the total token cost is already known, it is returned immediately.
If uncached, the system token cost, tool token cost, and each per-turn token cost are retrieved in sequence (each triggering its own computation logic if uncached). The sum of all retrieved values is stored as the total token cost and returned.
The total is stored regardless of whether any component costs used bytes-based estimates rather than provider counts. On the next request for the total, the stored value is returned without recomputing any component — even if those components are still individually marked uncached and would produce a different result if re-queried.
behavior.recording-known-turn-cost: A confirmed token count for a specific turn can be stored directly (used after turn completion when the response carries an authoritative token delta). If the turn index is greater than or equal to the active turn count, the process terminates abnormally. The stored value replaces any previously cached value for that turn, and the total token cost is reset to uncached.
behavior.invalidation:
Four operations reset cached values to uncached:
All component costs — system, tools, every per-turn entry, and total — are reset to uncached. The turn count, context start index, and pruned turn count are not changed.
All component costs are reset to uncached. Additionally, the active turn count is set to 0, the context start message index is set to 0, and the pruned turn count is set to 0. Per-turn array capacity is not changed.
The system token cost and the total token cost are reset to uncached.
The tool token cost and the total token cost are reset to uncached.
behavior.adding-a-turn:
When a new turn slot is appended, a new uncached entry is added to the per-turn array. If the array is already at capacity, its capacity is doubled before adding the entry.
If the current capacity is 0 at the time of growth, the new capacity is set to 8.
The active turn count is incremented by 1. The total token cost is reset to uncached.
behavior.pruning-oldest-turn:
When the oldest active turn is removed from context:
If the active turn count is 0, nothing happens.
If both the total token cost and the oldest turn's token cost are known, the oldest turn's cost is subtracted from the total. If the result is negative, the total is reset to uncached.
If either value is uncached, the total is reset to uncached.
The context start message index advances to the index of the second user-role message encountered starting from the current context start index. If no second user-role message exists in the agent's message list from that point forward, the context start index is set to the agent's total message count (one past the last message).
The pruned turn count is incremented by 1.
All per-turn cost entries are shifted one position toward index 0, removing the entry that was at index 0. The active turn count is decremented by 1.
behavior.removing-turns-from-index:
When turns from a given index onward (inclusive) are removed:
If the turn index is strictly greater than the active turn count, the process terminates abnormally.
If the turn index equals the active turn count, nothing happens.
For each turn being removed — from the given index to the last active turn — if that turn's cost is uncached, the total is reset to uncached and iteration stops. If the turn's cost is known and the total is currently known, that cost is subtracted from the total; if the result is negative, the total is reset to uncached.
The active turn count is set to the given index.
If the active turn count is now 0 and the context start message index is greater than 0, the context start message index is reset to 0 and the pruned turn count is reset to 0.
If the active turn count reaches 0 but the context start message index is already 0 (no pruning had ever occurred), the pruned turn count is not touched and remains at whatever value it held — though this value is also 0 in normal operation.
behavior.budget-operations: A token budget value can be read or set at any time. Reading returns the current budget without any side effects. Setting stores the new value immediately. No validation is performed on the supplied budget value.
behavior.non-computing-introspection:
Several read operations return state without triggering any computation:
Returns the active turn count.
Returns the pruned turn count, which is the absolute index of the oldest active turn relative to the full history.
Returns the context start message index: the position in the agent's message list of the oldest message currently included in active context.
Returns the cached total if known, or 0 if uncached. Does not compute.
Returns the cached cost for a specific turn index if known, or 0 if uncached. If the turn index is greater than or equal to the active turn count, the process terminates abnormally. Does not compute.

state-transitions.transition: System token cost changes from uncached to the provider count; total resets to uncached.

transition.trigger: system cost retrieved via bytes fallback

state-transitions.transition: System token cost remains uncached; total is unchanged.
Tool token cost changes from uncached to the provider count; total resets to uncached.
Tool token cost remains uncached; total is unchanged.
That turn's cost changes from uncached to the provider count; total resets to uncached.

transition.trigger: turn cost retrieved via bytes fallback

state-transitions.transition: That turn's cost remains uncached; total is unchanged.
Total token cost changes from uncached to the sum of all current component costs.
That turn's cost updated to the given value; total resets to uncached.
Active turn count increases by 1; new turn entry is uncached; total resets to uncached. Array capacity may double.
Active turn count decreases by 1; per-turn array shifts left; context start index advances to next user-role message; pruned turn count increases by 1; total is adjusted or reset to uncached.
All component costs and total reset to uncached.
All costs reset to uncached; turn count, context start index, and pruned turn count all set to 0.
